stem: A pupil adds the two 8-bit numbers 10110101 and 01011110 using binary addition.
model_answer: Add column-by-column from the right: 1+0=1, 0+1=1, 1+1=10 (write 0, carry 1), 0+1+1=10 (write 0, carry 1), 1+1+1=11 (write 1, carry 1), 1+0+1=10 (write 0, carry 1), 0+1+1=10 (write 0, carry 1), 1+0+1=10 (write 0, carry 1). The 9th-bit carry exceeds 8 bits, so the result is reported as overflow. The 8-bit answer recorded would be 00010011 with the carry-out flag set.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for: column-by-column addition method, recognition of carries, identification of 9th-bit overflow on this 8-bit sum.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain how to perform 10110101 + 01011110 in binary, and state whether overflow has occurred.

mark_points.text: adds column-by-column from the right (least-significant bit)
carries a 1 into the next column whenever the column total is 2 or more
identifies overflow because the sum produces a 9th bit beyond the 8-bit register

misconceptions.label: Overflow ignored
misconceptions.description: Forgetting to mention the 9th-bit carry-out leaves the answer incomplete — register width matters.
